title: Buttery Dense Slovakian White Cabbage Stir-fry
description: A sweet and savory brunch dish featuring white cabbage in a buttery sauce that's perfect for those looking to add a touch of Eastern European flavor to their meal.

ingredients.name: White cabbage
ingredients.notes: Trimmed and cut into bite-sized pieces

ingredients.name: Chicken breast
ingredients.notes: Marinated with a mix of olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper for about 30 minutes

ingredients.name: White wine vinegar
ingredients.notes: Use a white variety like pinot grigio

ingredients.name: Sugar-free soy sauce (or tamari)
ingredients.notes: To add depth and umami to the dish

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: To prevent sticking and for adding a bit of extra richness

instructions: Season the chicken with salt, pepper, and minced garlic.
Place the marinated chicken on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Roast in the oven for about 18-20 minutes or until cooked through. Remove from oven and set aside to cool slightly.
While the chicken is roasting, melt the butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Once melted, add the white wine vinegar and stir until it thickens slightly, around 2-3 minutes.
Add the sliced cabbage into the skillet with the simmering sauce. Season with salt to taste.
Stir occasionally for about 5-7 minutes or until the cabbage is soft and translucent but not mushy.
When the chicken is cool enough to handle, slice it into thin strips.
Combine the cooked chicken pieces with the stir-fried cabbage in a mixing bowl. Gently toss to combine all ingredients.
Divide the mixture evenly among four plates or bowls. Garnish with finely chopped parsley or dill and serve warm.
Optional: For added flavor, you can gently reheat the chicken before serving for extra warmth.
